chunk_summary: This section focuses on dietary assessment and feeding skills evaluation, emphasizing clinic-to-community coordination using Anganwadi POSHAN and NRC services for managing malnutrition and growth concerns in children.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of Anganwadi POSHAN and NRC services in community-based malnutrition management.
Learn how to create and communicate a one-page dietary plan for caregivers and community workers.
Identify referral thresholds for severe acute malnutrition (SAM) and medical instability.
Develop follow-up strategies based on risk levels and dietary progress.
key_facts: Families enrolled in ICDS can access regular growth monitoring and counseling at Anganwadi centers.
POSHAN Tracker is widely used in India to record anthropometry and services.
Referral thresholds include severe wasting, bilateral pitting edema, dehydration, or persistent vomiting.
Follow-up intervals should be 2–4 weeks for growth faltering, sooner for poor intake or safety concerns.
Rapid diet quality screens and feeding observations are essential for tracking progress and timely escalations.
reference_quote: Families enrolled in ICDS can be guided to regular growth monitoring and counseling at the Anganwadi, and many states use the POSHAN Tracker to record anthropometry and services.
document_key_topics: Dietary assessment and feeding skills evaluation
Clinic-to-community coordination using Anganwadi POSHAN services
Referral thresholds for severe acute malnutrition (SAM)
Follow-up strategies for growth monitoring and malnutrition management
